  • [ - ] JohnBenjamin - member since 24-Sep-16

    They will do the same as against the US: something called Provocative pressure. That means that you don't put pressure on the central defenders, but high up on the wings. At the same time you open up a corridor in the center, The defenders will play the ball through the center and the Dutch are waiting there to conquer the ball. The wingbacks who are higher up to give pressure, are now in place to receive the ball and counter. For the average watcher, it looks as if the other side has the upper hand, but if they are not careful, they are running into the Dutch knives. Good solid tactics from vGaal against the USA. Masterclass in effective football.

    3 years ago
     +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us

  • [ - ] JohnBenjamin - member since 24-Sep-16

    @hellreaper I don't know what match you watched against USA, but not the same as me. The US had hardly any serious chance; some shots from a distance. They did for sure not dominate the Dutch. I think you missed the last 5 years of development in football. What the Dutch did is called: provocative pressure. That means that you don't put pressure on the central defenders, but high up on the wings. At the same time you open up a corridor in the center, The defenders will play the ball through the center and the Dutch are waiting there to conquer the ball. The wingbacks who are higher up to give pressure, are now in place to receive the ball and counter. Good solid tactics from vGaal. Masterclass in effective football.

  • [ - ] StickandMove - member since 02-Oct-15

    Argentina, please! Punish the Netherlands for the arrogant attitude they have. A well-known Dutch soccer talk show described Argentina as a shabby team. It is typically Dutch. First talking down their own team and when they get a few performances against actually shabby teams speaking highly of themselves. Messi was called an old man. My view? They will be evenly matched. Holland has a very solid team and compared to the past they don't need to have the ball. They lurk on counterattacks and gamble on the three attacker up front. The wing defenders also play an important role in the changeover. Argentina has the best player of all time with Messi. The team plays in service of him, but there are certainly more good players. Di Maria is an example of that and they also have a player with a future: Alvarez. They have killers and fighters at the back and in midfield. I am thinking of a 0-0, 1-1, 0-1 or 1-2. I hope for a starring role for Messi.

    3 years ago

  • [ - ] StickandMove - member since 02-Oct-15

    @Roos The Dutch are overall a great contender on the World Cup. However they haven't won a single title. Their actions don't speak as loud as their words. For example: v. Gaal told the press aheah of the Ecuador game that their opponent changed their formation. They would adapt themselves to the Dutch formation, 5-3-2 or 3-5-2. V. Gaal then said he was happy to see the change, because (in his view) his team are the better side in that formation. During the game Ecuador was the better team on the pitch. V. Gaal also said once that their is no team that can play the 5-3-2 or 3-5-2 better than the Dutchies. If I'm not mistaking the Italians won the European Cup playing this formation. When was the last time the Netherlands won a tournament? Right, 1988. That year they won the European Cup. They should - in my humble opinion - be a little bit more humble themselves and talk with more respect about for example Argentina. They are the reigning champions of COMMEBOL.

  • [ - ] Icarax - member since 27-Sep-17

    australias 3 big chances: weak clearance by argentina, great run, great cross on the back post. if argentina can avoid mistakes like the first one especially, or alternatively dont flop chances, theyll win here. strong attack (scored 2 goals in all matches except unlucky first one) with great individual quality by stars like messi, alvarez, di maria, and decent collective play with passes by messi, fullbacks used to the fullest, and the team is strong to its core due to the energy of the midfield and decisiveness of the defenders especially on the first touch which stops A LOT of attacks. conceded 3 in 4 but 1 was a worldie and 1 was a deflection that was going out. netherlands allow enemies decent space, gakpo is the only strong performing attacker, overall defence conceding more chances and attack more soulless (though some great direct passes made em look dangerous) than argentina and much has to do with the midfield not being as present (though ofc not too easy to navigate through)
